_comment2: This skiplist is intentionally minimal: most tests pass when Preact renders
_comment2b: directly in jsdom without the dual-thread pipeline.
_comment3b: - Dual-thread: refs return DOM nodes, useLayoutEffect is synchronous
_comment3c: - Test methodology: getLog/clearLog spy on synchronous DOM, __pipelineRender gone
_comment3d: - BSI/snapshot machinery: none of it is needed here

tests: handle shuffled (stress test)
should effectively iterate on large lists
should effectively iterate on large component lists
skip_list.comment: Stress tests: exceed default test timeout under PAPI rendering overhead.

tests: should set enumerable boolean attribute
should not throw when changing contentEditable to undefined or null
should allow setting contentEditable to false
skip_list.comment: Web DOM IDL property: jsdom does not fully implement contentEditable as an IDL attribute (returns undefined instead of 'inherit'/'true'). The Lynx fork uses setAttribute/removeAttribute which sets the content attribute, but the IDL getter is not reflected by jsdom.

tests: should support (got|lost)PointerCapture events
skip_list.comment: Event name case normalization: the Lynx fork normalizes onGotPointerCapture to 'GotPointerCapture' (uppercase), while the test expects 'gotpointercapture' (lowercase). This is a difference in the forked Preact's event name handling vs upstream.

tests: should invoke refs in __lynxRender()
should not call stale refs
should invoke refs in Component.render()
should only null refs after unmount
should null and re-invoke refs when swapping component root element type
should add refs to components representing DOM nodes with no attributes if they have been pre-rendered
should not remove refs for memoized components keyed
should not remove refs for memoized components unkeyed
should properly call null for memoized components keyed
should properly call null for memoized components unkeyed
should bind refs before componentDidMount
should call refs after element is added to document on initial mount
should call refs after element is added to document on update
is invoked after refs are set
parent and child refs should be set before all effects
skip_list.comment: PAPI ref mismatch: ref.current is a LynxElement Proxy wrapping the jsdom element, not the jsdom HTMLElement itself. Tests that compare ref.current with scratch.firstChild (a raw jsdom element) will fail because they are different objects. In production Lynx, ref.current would be a native element handle.

tests: should correctly display DOM
should invoke layout effects after subtree is fully connected
should run layout affects after all refs are invoked
skip_list.comment: document.body.contains(ref.current) fails: ref.current is a LynxElement Proxy, not a jsdom Node, so jsdom's Node.contains() rejects it with 'parameter 1 is not of type Node'.

tests: should support subsequent renders w/ defaultValue
should support subsequent renders w/ defaultChecked
should not hydrate with dangerouslySetInnerHTML
should remove attributes on pre-existing DOM
skip_list.comment: Pre-existing DOM incompatibility: these tests set scratch.innerHTML before rendering to simulate DOM reuse (hydration). Our __kids array only tracks elements appended via PAPI; it does not see pre-existing jsdom children. So Preact's excessDomChildren list is empty and it creates new elements rather than reusing the existing ones.

tests: should support the <template> tag
skip_list.comment: template element: Preact renders children into template.content (a DocumentFragment), but our LynxElement does not expose a .content property. This is a niche HTML feature not relevant to Lynx apps.

tests: should not clear falsy DOM properties
skip_list.comment: DOM property path: test expects border={false} on <table> to produce border="false" attribute. In browsers, 'border' in table is true so Preact uses the DOM property path (table.border = false → 'false' string). For our LynxElement, 'border' is not a declared property so Preact uses setAttribute('border', false) which our applyViaElementPAPI converts to removeAttribute.
